## JS declaration emit now uses `export declare` instead of bare `export` (`declare` is optional and moot in declarations in .d.ts files)
## e.g. `export class Foo` → `export declare class Foo`, `export function f` → `export declare function f`

## JS declaration emit splits `export const/function` into `declare` + separate `export {}` statement (`declare const` + `export { x }` is semantically equivalent to `export const`)
## e.g. `export const x: 1` → `declare const x = 1; export { x };`

conformance/nodeModulesAllowJs1(module=node18).js.diff

conformance/nodeModulesAllowJs1(module=nodenext).js.diff

## Comma expressions in emitted JS are now parenthesized for correctness (parenthesizing comma expressions prevents precedence bugs)
## `a, b` in computed properties and JSX context now emitted as `(a, b)`

## Unnecessary parentheses removed from emitted JS and declarations (removing redundant parentheses is a safe simplification)
## Redundant parenthesization stripped from instantiation expressions, optional chain emit, and declaration types

## Parenthesization added/changed in declaration emit for complex types (added parentheses are required for correct parsing of these types)
## `keyof infer U extends number` → `keyof (infer U extends number)`, `T | T & undefined` → `T | (T & undefined)`, `readonly readonly string[]` → `readonly (readonly string[])`
conformance/inferTypesWithExtends1.js.diff

conformance/readonlyArraysAndTuples.js.diff

## Declaration emit preserves accessor form rather than merging to a single property type

## JSDoc type resolution changes: Object<K,V> → Record, ? → any|null, function() → Function (Documented in CHANGES.md in section "JSDoc Tags and Types")

## Nullable rest tuple member syntax changed from ...?T to ...T | null (`...T | null` is standard syntax; `...?T` was a non-standard printer shorthand)
## Named tuple member `...?string[]` now prints as `...string[] | null`
conformance/namedTupleMembersErrors.js.diff
conformance/restTupleElements1.js.diff

## Re-export statements split from single combined line into separate per-specifier lines (split re-exports are semantically equivalent; same names exported from same source)
## `export { default, foo, bar } from "x"` → three separate `export { ... } from "x"` statements

## BOM character handling fixed (bug fix; Strada was incorrectly mangling the BOM)
## BOM is now correctly preserved in output instead of being mangled, and spurious TS1127 errors are removed

## `new new Date` now emits `new (new Date)` with explicit parentheses (explicit parentheses ensure correct new expression precedence)
## Parenthesization added for `new` expression precedence correctness
compiler/newOperator.js.diff

## Import alias `import I = M` now additionally emits `var I = M` in JS output (emitting the variable assignment is more correct for runtime usage)
## Namespace import aliases in wrong context now emit JS variable assignment

compiler/moduleElementsInWrongContext2.js.diff

## Duplicate variable declarations removed from JS emit (redundant declarations are dead code; removing them is a safe cleanup)
## Redundant `var console;` or `let x;` declarations no longer emitted when namespace and variable share a name
compiler/module_augmentExistingVariable.js.diff
compiler/nameCollisions.js.diff

## Empty namespace IIFE no longer emitted (empty IIFEs are dead code with no runtime effect)
## `namespace global {}` inside a class no longer generates an empty IIFE in JS output
compiler/nestedGlobalNamespaceInClass.js.diff

## Inline JSDoc parameter comments no longer emitted in function type declarations (comments are cosmetic; type signatures are identical)
## Comments like `/** fooFunctionValue param */` stripped from declaration output
compiler/commentsFunction(target=es2015).js.diff

## JSDoc @typedef descriptions preserved inline instead of being converted to per-property comments (comment format change only; the type structure is identical)
## Full `@typedef`/`@property` JSDoc blocks are now preserved with the type alias
conformance/jsDeclarationsTypedefDescriptionsPreserved.js.diff

## `declare` modifier on import correctly elides the JS emit (`declare` imports are type-only and should not produce runtime code)
## `import = b` with `declare` modifier no longer emits `var a = b` in JS
## Input was declare import a = b;
compiler/declareModifierOnImport1.js.diff
## Input was declare export import a = x.c;
compiler/importDeclWithDeclareModifier.js.diff

## `import {} from "./a"` added for type-only import side effects (empty import has no effect on module exports or type signatures)
## Type-only imports with side effects now emit an empty import statement
conformance/bundlerSyntaxRestrictions(module=esnext).js.diff
conformance/bundlerSyntaxRestrictions(module=preserve).js.diff

## Unused `const _super = Object.create(null, {})` removed from async generator emit (dead code elimination; the `_super` binding was unused)
## Dead code in async methods with super access eliminated
conformance/asyncMethodWithSuper_es6.js.diff

## `accessor` keyword erroneously added to constructor in error recovery emit (input is invalid; both outputs are wrong, so the difference is immaterial)
## Invalid `accessor` modifier leaks onto `constructor()` in JS output
conformance/autoAccessorDisallowedModifiers(target=esnext).js.diff

## Mapped type with missing type now emits `any` instead of empty/missing in declaration (`any` is a safer fallback for incomplete types than empty/missing)
## Error recovery for incomplete mapped types improved
compiler/mappedTypeNoTypeNoCrash.js.diff

## @callback typedef now emitted as exported type in JS declaration emit (same type structure; callback typedef and class exports are equivalent)
## @callback constructs on constructors restructured with `export declare class` and explicit constructor

## `export {}` statement moved from end to beginning of JS output file (position of the module marker is cosmetic; no runtime effect)

## Numeric string property key uses computed property syntax ["404"] instead of plain "404" (`"404"` and `["404"]` declare the same property; consumers access it identically)
## Declaration emit changes how numeric-string-keyed properties are represented

## Non-literal computed property [fieldName] changed to index signature [x: string] in declaration emit (fieldName is not a legal const to use in a computed position, so this is an error case)
## Dynamic computed property keys resolved to index signatures
compiler/declarationEmitSimpleComputedNames1.js.diff

## `typeof` references resolved to concrete types in declaration emit (concrete types are equivalent to what `typeof` resolves to)
## `typeof a` rest parameters and symbol references resolved to tuple/symbol types instead of typeof
compiler/declarationEmitTypeofRest.js.diff

## Namespace-qualified names simplified to unqualified where context allows (unqualified names are correct when already in scope; simplification reduces noise)
## `me.Things<me.Props>` → `Things<Props>` when namespace context makes qualification unnecessary
compiler/defaultDeclarationEmitShadowedNamedCorrectly.js.diff

## Declaration emit uses import("...") type instead of namespace-qualified references (`import("./file").B` resolves to the same type as the namespace-qualified `A.B`)
## `A.B` changed to `import("./file").B` in some declaration contexts

compiler/declarationEmitNameConflicts.js.diff

## Properties with __proto__-like names now quoted in declaration emit (it's quoted in the originating sourcefile)

## Nested /*elided*/ type annotation comments removed at deeper nesting levels (`/*elided*/` comments are internal artifacts with no semantic meaning)
## Declaration emit no longer preserves elided comments in nested class expressions

## Invalid @template defaults handled differently
conformance/jsdocTemplateTagDefault.js.diff

## @template-tagged function now emits as const arrow type instead of function declaration (`declare function f<T>()` and `declare const f: <T>() => R` are equivalent callable types)
